errors: The ELM implementation is missing the check for a qualifying encounter during the measurement period (from "Encounter to Screen for Depression" value set 2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.1916) in the inclusion criteria. The CPG requires this encounter to be present for the patient to be in the target population, but the ELM's "Needs Depression Screening" logic does not include this condition. All other numeric values (age thresholds, time intervals) and value set references match the CPG requirements.
[Age threshold mismatch: CPG requires age >= 18 but ELM uses age >= 25. Time interval mismatch: CPG requires 12-month lookback for blood pressure encounters but ELM uses 6-month lookback.]
[Age range upper limit mismatch: CPG specifies 85 years but ELM implements 80 years]
[HbA1c threshold value mismatch: CPG specifies HbA1c >= 6.5%, but ELM implementation uses 9 g%. The numeric value does not match exactly.]

[The ELM implementation does not include the pack-year smoking history (20 pack-years) or quit time within 15 years criteria in the "Has Smoking History" logic. The CPG requires these specific smoking history parameters for inclusion, but the ELM only checks for the presence of a "Tobacco User" condition without verifying pack-years or quit duration.]

[Age thresholds in ELM implementation are incorrect (60-75 years) compared to CPG requirements (45-75 years). Time interval for recent colorectal screening is 2 years in ELM but should be 1 year per CPG requirements.]

[Time interval for recent anxiety screening is 6 months in ELM implementation, but CPG requires 12 months (1 year) lookback period. The value set reference is correct, but the time duration mismatch affects eligibility determination.]

[Age thresholds in ELM implementation do not match CPG requirements. CPG specifies age 15-65, but ELM uses age >=30. Additionally, ELM does not include the upper age limit of 65 years. The "In Demographic" logic in ELM is incorrectly defined as age >=30 instead of 15-65. The "Needs HIV Screening" logic should check age between 15-65, not just age >=30.]
[BMI threshold in ELM is 35 kg/m2 instead of 25 kg/m2 as required by CPG; recent glucose test lookback period in ELM is 1 year instead of 3 years as required by CPG]

[Age thresholds in ELM implementation do not match CPG requirements. CPG specifies age 21-65 for inclusion, but ELM's "In Demographic" logic uses age >=30 instead of >=21. This creates a mismatch in the lower age boundary for screening eligibility.]
[Age threshold in ELM implementation is 75 years, but CPG requires 65 years. This mismatch in age criteria (65 vs 75) affects the In Demographic logic and subsequent InPopulation determination.]

[Age threshold in ELM implementation is 25 years (In Demographic: age >=25) but CPG requires age >=18 years. This creates a mismatch in inclusion criteria where patients aged 18-24 would be incorrectly excluded from screening eligibility.]

The ELM implementation incorrectly specifies a 6-month lookback period for recent DEXA scans (Has Recent DEXA Scan) instead of the 2-year requirement stated in the CPG. The CPG explicitly requires "no DEXA scan in the past 2 years" for screening eligibility, but the ELM uses a 6-month interval.

[In Demographic age threshold is incorrectly set to >=55 years instead of >=65 years as specified in CPG requirements. The ELM implementation uses "Age >=55" while the CPG requires "Age >=65" for inclusion criteria.]

The logical structure in the ELM implementation incorrectly uses an OR operator instead of an AND operator for combining Screening Age and Gender in the MeetsInclusionCriteria. The CPG requires **ALL** inclusion criteria (age >=40 AND female AND no mammogram in past 1 year) to be true, but the ELM uses `(Screening Age OR Gender) AND Mammogram`, which would incorrectly include patients who are either age >=40 or female (not both). This misalignment violates the CPG's explicit requirement for all inclusion criteria to be satisfied.

[The ELM implementation incorrectly defines "Needs Tobacco Use Screening" as only checking age (In Demographic) without combining it with the absence of recent tobacco screening. The CPG requires "MeetsInclusionCriteria" to be true only if **both** age >=18 **and** no recent screening (within 1 year) are satisfied. The ELM's "InPopulation" logic fails to enforce this AND condition, leading to incorrect eligibility determination.]

[1. The ELM implementation only checks age >= 50 years but is missing the upper age limit of 80 years from the CPG requirements. 2. The ELM implementation does not include the 20 pack-year smoking history requirement or the 15-year quit window in the "Has Smoking History" logic. 3. The logical structure for "In Demographic" is incomplete as it only checks age >=50 without the upper age bound. 4. The "Has Smoking History" logic only checks for tobacco user status but does not verify the 20 pack-year requirement or 15-year quit window.]

The ELM implementation is missing the exclusion check for active Bipolar Disorder diagnosis in the Needs Depression Screening logic. The CPG requires checking for no active Bipolar Disorder diagnosis (value set 2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.450) as part of inclusion criteria, but this is not included in the ELM's Needs Depression Screening calculation. The ELM only checks In Demographic, NOT Has Depression Diagnosis, and NOT Has Recent Screening, but omits the Bipolar Disorder exclusion.

[The ELM implementation incorrectly inverts the logic for the Colonoscopy condition. The CPG requires that no colonoscopy exists in the past 1 month (count = 0) to meet inclusion criteria. However, the ELM defines "Colonoscopy" as COUNT(...) > 0, which would incorrectly include patients with a recent colonoscopy in the InPopulation. The correct logic should check for COUNT(...) = 0 to align with the CPG requirement.]

[The ELM implementation incorrectly uses "AND Has Recent Falls Assessment" in the "Needs Falls Prevention Screening" logic, whereas the CPG requires "AND NOT Has Recent Falls Assessment". This inverts the decision logic, leading to incorrect screening recommendations when a recent falls risk assessment exists.]

[The ELM implementation incorrectly combines the exclusion criteria for Bipolar and Depression Diagnoses. The CPG requires both exclusions to be true (AND), but the ELM uses NOT (Has Bipolar Diagnosis AND Has Depression Diagnosis), which logically allows exclusion if either diagnosis is present. The correct logic should be NOT Has Bipolar Diagnosis AND NOT Has Depression Diagnosis. This logical error would incorrectly include patients with either diagnosis in the population needing screening.]

The value set reference for "Fall Risk Assessment" in the ELM implementation (`2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.564`) does not match the CPG-specified value set (`2.16.840.1.113883.3.464.1003.118.12.1028`). This mismatch could lead to incorrect identification of falls risk assessments. All other numeric values (age threshold of 65, 1-year lookback period) and logical operators (AND/NOT) align with the CPG requirements.

The value set used in the ELM implementation for "Alcohol Use Screening" (2.16.840.1.113883.3.526.3.1278) does not match the value set specified in the CPG requirements (2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.1542). All other numeric values (age threshold of 18 years, 1-year lookback period) and logical operators (AND, NOT) align with the CPG requirements.
